 {"id":"bd-2id5","title":"Files: error handling + permissions UX","description":"Polish the Files page by handling real-world filesystem failure cases gracefully.

Cases to handle (minimum):
- Permission denied (cannot read dir / cannot read file)
- Broken symlink / disappeared file (race)
- Binary file (non-UTF8 or mostly non-printable)
- Huge file (avoid loading entire file into memory)

UX requirements:
- Never panic.
- Show a polished inline error state in the preview pane (icon + short message + next step).
- Emit a toast/log entry with the underlying error for debugging.
- Provide safe fallbacks:
  - binary: show "binary" notice + optional hex/summary view (stretch)
  - huge: show first N bytes/lines + "truncated" indicator

Acceptance:
- Errors are actionable and aesthetically consistent with the rest of the app.
- The user always has a way to recover (navigate away, retry, change directory).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:21.751994341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:34.189726351Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","files"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2id5","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:25.777641935Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2id5","depends_on_id":"bd-201t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:21.775953041Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2id5","depends_on_id":"bd-fdf3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:23.100717606Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2k35","title":"demo_showcase core: multi-pane app chrome (header/sidebar/main/footer)","description":"Implement the overall layout shell.

Requirements:
- Header: app title, env/status indicators, time, theme name.
- Sidebar: navigation + optional filter/search.
- Main: active page content.
- Footer: contextual help / key hints / transient notifications.

Acceptance:
- Layout reflows on window resize (initially crude is fine; INTERACT epic polishes it).
- No page-specific layout hacks: pages render within provided content area.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:57.403212904Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:40:43.247269110Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:40:43.247203398Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase","layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2k35","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:57.424361535Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2k35","depends_on_id":"bd-3mh2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:16.989561358Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2k35","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:15.785608900Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2kp1","title":"Settings: about/credits + diagnostics","description":"Add a polished About + Diagnostics section inside Settings.

Why:
- This is *real product* behavior (every serious ops tool has an About/Diagnostics panel).
- It doubles as a developer-facing debug surface and a user-facing confidence signal.

Diagnostics content (at minimum):
- demo_showcase version + build info (crate versions for bubbletea/lipgloss/bubbles/glamour/huh/harmonica/wish/charmed_log)
- active runtime Config (theme name, seed, toggles like mouse/animations/no-color/no-alt-screen)
- terminal info (detected color profile, TERM/COLORTERM, width/height last known)

Nice-to-have (if available cheaply):
- feature flags enabled (async, ssh, syntax)
- recent error/notification summary

Actions:
- "Copy diagnostics" (to clipboard if feasible, otherwise export to file)
- "Open diagnostics in pager" using the shell-out flow (bubbletea release/restore + sequence)

Acceptance:
- Diagnostics are readable, well-formatted, and update when Config/theme changes.
- Actions never hang in CI/headless mode; failures become toasts/logs.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:09.931592707Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:46.057423922Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","settings"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kp1","depends_on_id":"bd-194c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:57:58.531010722Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kp1","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:46.057393756Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kp1","depends_on_id":"bd-274o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:10.201659035Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kp1","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:57:56.044885213Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kp1","depends_on_id":"bd-3s4n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:57:57.301987137Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kp1","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:13.453221221Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2lkz","title":"Docs: in-doc search + match navigation","description":"Add search within the current Docs page (like a pager/reader).

Why:
- Search is a make-or-break capability for terminal docs viewers.
- It showcases textinput + highlight rendering + keybinding conventions.

Requirements:
- `/` enters search mode (focuses a query input).
- Highlights matches in the rendered doc output.
- `n`/`N` jumps to next/previous match.
- Show match count and current match index (e.g., "3/12").

Performance:
- Do not require an expensive full markdown re-render on every keystroke.
- Prefer searching within the already-rendered text buffer and applying highlight overlays.

Testability:
- The search/match navigation logic should be unit tested via the shared filtering/search engine tests.
- Covered by the Docs E2E scenario.

Acceptance:
- Feels like a real pager: fast, predictable, keyboard-centric.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:44.495821283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:23.489705254Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2lkz","depends_on_id":"bd-22vc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:44.511693330Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2lkz","depends_on_id":"bd-28jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:33.889650563Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2lkz","depends_on_id":"bd-3jg2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:31.319028559Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2lkz","depends_on_id":"bd-i0i9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:28.626427229Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2mvl","title":"Quality: snapshot tests for key views","description":"Add snapshot-style tests for key rendered views so UI regressions are caught early.

Why:
- TUIs are easy to break visually (alignment, borders, truncation) with small refactors.
- Snapshots complement state-based tests by asserting the actual rendered output.

What to snapshot (minimum):
- Dashboard (80x24)
- Jobs list/table (80x24)
- Logs (a few entries, follow mode on/off)
- Docs (one doc rendered at two widths)
- Settings (toggles + theme picker)

Snapshot strategy:
- Prefer ASCII/no-color snapshots for stability.
- If we snapshot colored output, normalize to deterministic sequences or snapshot via the "export plain" path.
- Keep snapshots small and focused (don’t snapshot megabytes of logs).

Acceptance:
- Snapshots are deterministic in CI.
- Failures are diff-friendly and point to the exact view that changed.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:28.393184434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:30:23.677208502Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","insta","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2mvl","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:28.411924655Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2mvl","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:21.323882037Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2mvl","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:18.666497689Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2mvl","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:23.910513817Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2myg","title":"Dashboard: live metrics + status indicators","description":"Wire dashboard widgets to the demo data model so the dashboard is *alive* (real-time updates, plausible trends, state changes).

Why:
- Static numbers look fake. Live metrics make the dashboard feel like a real ops tool.
- This also validates the simulation engine and the app’s tick/update architecture.

Requirements (minimum):
- Live-updating metrics:
  - requests/sec (or throughput)
  - p95 latency (or similar)
  - error rate
  - job throughput / queue depth
- Health/status indicators:
  - per-metric health state (ok/warn/error) derived from thresholds
  - trends (up/down/flat) based on deltas over a window
  - at least one periodic “incident” / degradation to prove the UI can surface issues
- Feedback:
  - state transitions produce a toast/alert + (optional) log entry
  - drill-down panel should explain *why* a metric is warn/error (thresholds, recent values)

Implementation notes:
- Metrics come from the deterministic simulation engine; use a seed in test/E2E.
- Avoid noisy/flappy states:
  - consider hysteresis or smoothing so warn/error doesn’t flicker on boundaries.

Acceptance:
- Values visibly change over time.
- Health transitions are obvious and explainable.
- No flicker/jank; dashboard remains responsive.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - health state computation (thresholds + hysteresis if used)
  - trend computation over a known sequence
  - “incident” generation is deterministic for a fixed seed
- E2E:
  - smoke tour sees changing values
  - settings/theme scenario confirms dashboard updates immediately after toggles.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:57.979377768Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:48:38.344449577Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:48:38.344380729Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:47:27.312380976Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:57.995055004Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:16.935347602Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:18.142227251Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw3","title":"lipgloss: Code Consolidation & ANSI Parsing Hardening","description":"# Epic: lipgloss Code Consolidation & ANSI Parsing Hardening

## Background
The lipgloss crate provides terminal styling (colors, borders, layout). During deep review,
code duplication and incomplete ANSI handling were identified as maintenance risks.

## Issues Identified

### 1. Three Independent visible_width() Implementations - HIGH
**Locations**:
- lib.rs lines 365-417: Full state machine with Normal, Esc, Csi, Osc states
- style.rs lines 1965-1996: Simplified two-state approach
- backend.rs lines 621-640: Even more simplified, no OSC handling

**Problem**: Each version has different completeness levels. They may produce different
results for the same input, causing layout inconsistencies.

**Risk**: Maintenance nightmare - bug fixes need to be applied to 3 places.

### 2. Incomplete OSC Sequence Handling - MEDIUM
**Location**: lib.rs lines 403-411
**Problem**: OSC sequence termination (ST = ESC \) is not properly validated.
The state machine transitions to Esc state on seeing ESC but doesn't verify
the following backslash character.

**Risk**: Malformed OSC sequences could cause incorrect width calculations.

### 3. Border Color Indexing Confusion - LOW
**Problem**: Border colors stored as [top, right, bottom, left] but accessed
inconsistently across different methods.

**Risk**: Wrong colors could be applied to wrong edges in edge cases.

## Design Philosophy
- Single Source of Truth: One canonical visible_width implementation
- Defensive Parsing: Handle all edge cases in ANSI parsing
- Clear Documentation: Document the expected format for all color arrays

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single visible_width() function used everywhere
- [ ] Complete ANSI escape sequence handling including OSC
- [ ] Consistent border color indexing throughout
- [ ] All changes have regression tests","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:16:49.723527312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:16:49.747276437Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ansi-parsing","code-quality","lipgloss"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw3","depends_on_id":"bd-gyzr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:16:49.747238416Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3ut7","title":"bubbletea: Use thread pool for batch command execution","description":"# Task: Replace Unbounded Thread Spawning with Thread Pool

## Problem Statement
Currently, batch commands spawn one OS thread per command:

```rust
// Lines 834-841
for cmd in batch.0 {
    let tx_clone = tx.clone();
    thread::spawn(move || {
        if let Some(msg) = cmd.execute() {
            let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);
        }
    });
}
```

This is problematic because:
1. OS thread creation is expensive (~1ms per thread)
2. Each thread consumes stack space (default 2MB on Linux)
3. No limit on concurrent threads
4. OS may reject thread creation under pressure

## Impact Analysis
A batch of 100 commands:
- Current: Spawns 100 threads, ~100ms overhead, ~200MB stack reservation
- With pool: Reuses N workers, ~1ms overhead, ~2N MB stack usage

## Solution Design

### Option A: Use rayon Thread Pool (RECOMMENDED)
```rust
use rayon::prelude::*;

// In handle_command for BatchMsg:
if let Some(batch) = msg.downcast::<BatchMsg>() {
    let tx_clone = tx.clone();
    rayon::spawn(move || {
        batch.0.into_par_iter().for_each(|cmd| {
            if let Some(msg) = cmd.execute() {
                let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);
            }
        });
    });
}
```

Benefits:
- Work-stealing scheduler for optimal CPU utilization
- Thread pool sized to CPU cores by default
- Battle-tested in production

### Option B: Dedicated Thread Pool
```rust
use std::sync::mpsc;
use std::thread;

struct CommandPool {
    workers: Vec<thread::JoinHandle<()>>,
    sender: mpsc::Sender<Box<dyn FnOnce() + Send>>,
}
```

More control but more code to maintain.

### Option C: Configurable Pool Size
Add configuration option:
```rust
impl Program<M> {
    pub fn with_batch_pool_size(mut self, size: usize) -> Self {
        self.batch_pool_size = Some(size);
        self
    }
}
```

## Implementation Steps
1. Add rayon as optional dependency (feature-gated)
2. Create command pool abstraction
3. Replace thread::spawn with pool submission
4. Add configuration for pool size
5. Add tests verifying bounded thread usage
6. Benchmark batch command performance

## Backward Compatibility
- Default behavior should be similar to current (parallel execution)
- Pool size defaults to num_cpus
- No API changes required

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn batch_commands_use_thread_pool() {
    let active_threads = Arc::new(AtomicUsize::new(0));
    let max_threads = Arc::new(AtomicUsize::new(0));
    
    // Create batch of 100 commands that track thread count
    let commands = (0..100).map(|_| {
        let active = active_threads.clone();
        let max = max_threads.clone();
        Command::new(move || {
            let current = active.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst) + 1;
            max.fetch_max(current, Ordering::SeqCst);
            std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(10));
            active.fetch_sub(1, Ordering::SeqCst);
            None
        })
    }).collect();
    
    let batch = Command::batch(commands);
    // Execute batch...
    
    // Should be bounded to pool size, not 100
    assert!(max_threads.load(Ordering::SeqCst) <= num_cpus::get() * 2);
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Batch commands execute via thread pool
- [ ] Pool size is configurable (default: num_cpus)
- [ ] No thread exhaustion with large batches
- [ ] Performance equal or better than current
- [ ] rayon is optional dependency (feature-gated)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:18:28.393528718Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:18:28.410770133Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","performance","thread-pool"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ut7","depends_on_id":"bd-2pex","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:18:28.410727754Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-4d5e","title":"E2E scenario: smoke tour (visit every page, no panics)","description":"Scenario: a deterministic smoke tour that proves the app boots, routes, and renders across the whole surface area.

Steps (headless/custom-IO):
- Start app with deterministic Config (fixed seed, animations off).
- Navigate to:
  - Dashboard
  - Jobs
  - Docs
  - Wizard
  - Files
  - Logs
  - Settings
- Open/close the help overlay.
- In Settings:
  - toggle no-color/ASCII mode and confirm ANSI escapes disappear from output
  - switch theme preset and confirm the visible theme indicator updates
- Quit cleanly.

Optional/conditional steps (skip if feature not present yet):
- Open command palette.
- Start guided tour mode.

Assertions:
- No panics and no hangs.
- App state transitions as expected (page headers match).
- Key UI strings appear in output (title, page headers, theme name label).

Acceptance:
- Runs fast and deterministically.

+{"id":"bd-4d5e","title":"E2E scenario: smoke tour (visit every page, no panics)","description":"Scenario: a deterministic smoke tour that proves the app boots, routes, and renders across the whole surface area.

Steps (headless/custom-IO):
- Start app with deterministic Config (fixed seed, animations off).
- Navigate to:
  - Dashboard
  - Jobs
  - Docs
  - Wizard
  - Files
  - Logs
  - Settings
- Open/close the help overlay.
- In Settings:
  - toggle no-color/ASCII mode and confirm ANSI escapes disappear from output
  - switch theme preset and confirm the visible theme indicator updates
- Quit cleanly.

Optional/conditional steps (skip if feature not present yet):
- Open command palette.
- Start guided tour mode.

Assertions:
- No panics and no hangs.
- App state transitions as expected (page headers match).
- Key UI strings appear in output (title, page headers, theme name label).

Acceptance:
- Runs fast and deterministically.
- Catches regressions in routing/layout/theme plumbing early.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:35.237974495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:19:01.714323165Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-1aad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:23.194963017Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-1nwu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:20.869742832Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-28l3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:13.866362166Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-2q4a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:07:51.168707692Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:08.439038707Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:12.230376916Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:10.963093037Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-fdf3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:16.132478669Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:09.690902812Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:18.554874272Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:35.267810523Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5hs1","title":"Wish: fix 28 unused_async clippy warnings in tests","description":"The wish/tests/e2e/ test files have 28 clippy::unused_async warnings that prevent cargo clippy --workspace -D warnings from passing cleanly.

Scope:
- Review each async function in crates/wish/tests/e2e/ and tests/common/mod.rs
- Either add await points where appropriate or remove async keyword
- Ensure tests still pass after changes
- Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings to verify fix","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:43:23.590032477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T16:50:12.350469495Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T16:50:12.350395978Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5hs1","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:43:23.596927485Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6wkl","title":"E2E scenario: mouse scroll + click-to-select","description":"Scenario: validate mouse scroll + click-to-select/focus behavior.

Why:
- Mouse support is a premium feature and easy to regress.
- This scenario proves bubbletea mouse plumbing + demo_showcase hit-testing/mapping is correct in a headless, deterministic way.

Steps (suggested):
- Start the app with mouse enabled.
- Navigate to a scrollable screen (Docs viewport or Files preview).
- Inject mouse wheel events to scroll down, then up.
- Navigate to a clickable/selection surface (Dashboard card grid or Jobs table).
- Inject a mouse click at known coordinates and assert selection/focus changes.
- (Optional) Toggle mouse off in Settings and assert wheel/click no longer changes state.

Assertions:
- Scroll wheel changes viewport offset (rendered output differs and/or a scroll indicator updates).
- Click selects the intended item (visible highlight/focus state changes deterministically).
- No panics; no runaway input loops.

Implementation notes:
- Prefer the E2E runner’s semantic DSL events (`MouseMsg`) for stability.
- If needed, add a “raw byte” mode sub-test that injects an xterm SGR mouse sequence to exercise the InputParser.

Acceptance:
- Catches regressions in mouse mapping and makes failures actionable (step id + last frame + artifact path).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:50:58.031885548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:21.100709336Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","mouse","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:17.148362656Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-3d1w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:21.100671155Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:19.756678314Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-3ps4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:18.467597486Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:50:58.052269553Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-7iul","title":"demo_showcase core: demonstrate bubbletea println/printf","description":"Use bubbletea `println`/`printf` in a controlled way.
